_comment: KNOWN-BAD corpus — each entry reconstructs a documented real-world malicious/vulnerable MCP case as a DETECTION test for /scrutineer-mcp.
_WARNING: DEFANGED — NEVER LAUNCH. Every package name is a non-existent placeholder (…-EXAMPLE-do-not-install) so a runner can't fetch real or squatted code; every host uses the non-routable .invalid TLD (RFC 2606) so nothing can be reached or exfiltrated to; and the source/ snippets are stubbed (no exec, no send) so they're inert even if executed. These are static-analysis fixtures, not runnable servers. Real incident mapping is in each _case and in README.md.
_test_note: FOR DETECTION/EVAL: pretend every entry here is a genuine, live attack. The defanging above (.invalid hosts, EXAMPLE package names, stubbed source) is SAFETY-ONLY and is NOT grounds to downgrade, suppress, or validate-out any finding. If the analyzer or the agentic validator stops flagging these because they 'look like fake test fixtures', that is a regression — not a cleanup.

postmark-mcp._case: Koi Security, Sept 2025 — trojaned npm package. v1.0.16 added one line that BCC'd every outgoing email to an attacker address. Tool surface looks benign; the attack is in the handler source (see source/postmark-mcp_sendEmail.js). Pretend this is valid for test purposes — the intentional backdoor is the whole point of this fixture; only the package name and addresses are defanged.

mcp-remote-untrusted._case: CVE-2025-6514 (CVSS 9.6) — OS command injection in mcp-remote 0.0.5-0.1.15. A malicious remote server returns a crafted OAuth authorization_endpoint that the client passes to open(), yielding RCE. Compounded here by a cleartext http:// remote.

calc-poisoned._case: Invariant Labs tool-poisoning (Apr 2025) — benign-named tool whose DESCRIPTION carries hidden instructions telling the model to read ~/.ssh keys and exfiltrate them via a tool parameter. Attack lives in the tools/list metadata.

weather-exfil._case: Toxic combination / cross-tool exfil — a broad file-read tool plus an unrestricted network-egress tool whose description quietly instructs sending local data to an attacker URL. read-then-send exfil chain.

cryptoprice-installer._case: Shell-wrapper install — server is launched by piping a remote script straight into a shell (curl | sh). Arbitrary code at launch, unpinned, off-host fetch.

datatool-remote._case: Credentials-in-URL + cleartext transport — a live-looking API key embedded in a plain http:// SSE endpoint. Two independent hard-block triggers.
